title: Best Magnesium for Sleep: Ranked by RCT Evidence (2026)
description: Four magnesium forms compete for the best-for-sleep title. Only one has a 2025 independent RCT. The ranking is built on bioavailability data, human trial evidence, and a transparent scoring rubric — not supplement labels.
seoTitle: Best Magnesium for Sleep: Ranked by RCT Evidence (2026)
seoDescription: Best magnesium supplements for sleep ranked by RCT evidence, bioavailability, and safety. Which form the 2025 bisglycinate trial supported and why oxide fails.
imageAlt: Four crystalline magnesium supplement forms ranked on a dark laboratory bench under focused light

ranked.name: Magnesium glycinate (bisglycinate)
ranked.summary: Only form with a dedicated independent 2025 sleep RCT (n=155, Cohen's d=0.2). GABA-A + glycine dual mechanism. Minimal GI effects.

ranked.name: Magnesium citrate
ranked.summary: High bioavailability verified in human crossover trial. Strong general-repletion form. No dedicated sleep RCT; laxative risk above 300 mg elemental.

ranked.name: Magnesium L-threonate (Magtein)
ranked.summary: One 2024 industry-funded RCT (n=80). Brain-preferential claim based on rat data. Mixed independent evidence. High cost.

ranked.name: Magnesium oxide
ranked.summary: Most common form in early sleep trials (Abbasi 2012). Approximately 4% bioavailability. High GI distress. Not recommended as first choice.
